examples.title: CBC Mode Encryption Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of CBC (Cipher Block Chaining) mode encryption
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CBC_Encrypt(plaintext, key, IV, blockCipher)
INPUT: plaintext array P[1..n], key K, IV, block cipher E
OUTPUT: ciphertext array C[1..n]

BEGIN
  FOR i FROM 1 TO n DO
    IF i = 1 THEN
      // First block: XOR with IV
      input_block ← IV ⊕ P[i]
    ELSE
      // Subsequent blocks: XOR with previous ciphertext
      input_block ← C[i-1] ⊕ P[i]
    END IF
    
    // Encrypt the combined block
    C[i] ← E(K, input_block)
  END FOR
  
  RETURN C
END
examples.explanation: CBC mode XORs each plaintext block with the previous ciphertext block before encryption. The first block uses an Initialization Vector (IV). This chaining ensures that identical plaintext blocks produce different ciphertext blocks.

examples.title: AES Encryption with CBC Mode
examples.code:
from Crypto.Cipher import AES
from Crypto.Random import get_random_bytes

def aes_cbc_encrypt(plaintext, key):
    # Generate random 16-byte IV
    iv = get_random_bytes(16)
    
    # Create cipher in CBC mode
    cipher = AES.new(key, AES.MODE_CBC, iv)
    
    # Pad plaintext to multiple of 16 bytes
    padding_length = 16 - (len(plaintext) % 16)
    plaintext += bytes([padding_length] * padding_length)
    
    # Encrypt
    ciphertext = cipher.encrypt(plaintext)
    
    return iv, ciphertext

def aes_cbc_decrypt(ciphertext, key, iv):
    # Create cipher for decryption
    cipher = AES.new(key, AES.MODE_CBC, iv)
    
    # Decrypt and remove padding
    plaintext = cipher.decrypt(ciphertext)
    padding_length = plaintext[-1]
    plaintext = plaintext[:-padding_length]
    
    return plaintext

# Example usage
key = get_random_bytes(32)  # 256-bit key
message = b"Hello, Security!"
iv, encrypted = aes_cbc_encrypt(message, key)
decrypted = aes_cbc_decrypt(encrypted, key, iv)
print(f"Original: {message}")
print(f"Decrypted: {decrypted}")
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates AES-256 encryption in CBC mode. It includes PKCS#7 padding, random IV generation, and proper decryption with padding removal.

examples.title: AES Key Schedule
examples.description: C code showing AES key expansion for 128-bit key
examples.explanation: This C code implements the AES key expansion algorithm. For AES-128, it expands a 128-bit (16-byte) key into 44 32-bit round keys (176 bytes total). The key schedule uses RotWord (rotation), SubWord (S-box substitution), and XOR with round constants (Rcon).

examples.title: CTR Mode Encryption
examples.description: Java implementation of Counter (CTR) mode encryption
examples.explanation: CTR mode generates a keystream by encrypting successive counter values (nonce + counter). Each plaintext block is XORed with the keystream. Advantages include parallelizable encryption/decryption and random access capability.
